narrative: In the afternoon, a chorus of Chestnut-backed Chickadees could be heard darting through the oak woodland, while Common Ravens called out from their vantage points on the ridgelines. Orange-crowned Warblers kept to themselves in dense brush, their olive-green plumage blending seamlessly into the chaparral and scrub. The open vistas provided by Tilden's ridge lines offered perfect views of these avian inhabitants.
